other_observers: none; I was not the original finder of this bird. Its presence had previously been reported on cobirds.
description: I observed the bird as it stood on an exposed shoreline in the open. Nearby, a juvenile Black-crowned Night-heron stood in a similar location, making for a great comparison.  Both were small, heron-like birds, similar in size to a Snowy Egret, but much stockier.  The Yellow-crowned Night-heron had a thicker bill (relative to its length). The end of the bill was quite rounded; the bill did not have the dagger-like appearance and sharp point of a Black-crowned.  The bill of the Yellow-crowned was all dark (blackish or dark brown). The lower mandible of the Black-crowned was mostly yellow, except near the tip.  The back and folded wings of the Yellow-crowned were a dark chocolate brown, compared to the light brown of the Black-crowned.  Both birds had bold triangular white spots on their wings, but only the Yellow-crowned had white edges to the feathers.  Both birds had dark pupils with bold orange irises. They had yellowish legs and boldly streaked brown and pale underparts.Behaviors: Standing on the shoreline of a wooded pond.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenile
